title: B1 Listening - Part 1 - Test 8
instructions: For each question, listen to the recording and choose the correct answer: A, B or C.

items.context: You hear two friends talking about a bus stop.
items.audio_script: Girl: Are we still meeting by the railway station for the journey home? I wrote it down yesterday, but I cannot find the message now. || Boy: That was the original plan, but the road near the station is closed this afternoon. [1]We need to meet outside the supermarket instead[/1]. || Girl: Thanks for telling me. I would have gone to the wrong place and wondered where everyone was. || Boy: No problem. I will arrive ten minutes early, so look for me near the entrance. Keep your phone on in case anything changes again.
items.question: Where should they catch the bus?
items.explanation: The bus leaves from outside the supermarket.

items.context: You hear two students preparing for a cooking class.
items.audio_script: Boy: Are you ready for the cooking class yet? I put an apron in my bag because I thought that was what we needed. || Girl: That was on the first list, but the school has bought new aprons for everyone to use. [1]Everyone needs to bring a small mixing bowl now[/1]. || Boy: Good thing I asked. I was about to leave my bag by the door and forget about it. || Girl: You still have time. Put it in now, and check the final message once more before we leave tomorrow morning.
items.question: What should the student bring?
items.explanation: The student should bring a mixing bowl.

items.context: You hear two friends arranging what to do after school.
items.audio_script: Girl: Are we still going to the library after school today? I told my sister I would be home later, so I need to know. || Boy: That was the idea, but the library is closing early for staff training. [1]We are going to practise football in the park instead[/1]. || Girl: That actually sounds better. I nearly paid for the other activity online this morning. || Boy: It is a good thing you had not booked anything yet. I will book our places this evening and send you the time. We can meet at the bus stop after lunch.
items.question: What will they do after school?
items.explanation: They will practise football in the park.

items.context: You hear two people talking about a coach journey.
items.audio_script: Man: I need to write down the time for the coach to Oxford. Is it still at half past eight, like you said yesterday? || Woman: No, the driver has been delayed on an earlier journey. [1]It starts at quarter to nine now[/1], although we should arrive ten minutes earlier. || Man: That is useful to know. I would have been waiting there at the wrong time. || Woman: I will send you a reminder later. Bring everything you need, because there will not be time to go home again before it begins.
items.question: What time does the coach leave?
items.explanation: The coach leaves at quarter to nine.

items.context: You hear two teenagers talking about a tablet.
items.audio_script: Woman: How are you getting on with your tablet? I heard it was not working properly. || Man: I first thought the screen had cracked, and my brother wondered if the volume button was broken. But he checked it carefully last night. [1]It keeps turning itself off after a few minutes[/1]. || Woman: Can it be fixed before your online lesson tomorrow? || Man: Yes, probably. We know what is wrong now, and the repair shop said it should be ready tomorrow afternoon. That is a relief because I need it this weekend.
items.question: What is the problem with the tablet?

items.context: You hear two people planning a journey to the seaside.
items.audio_script: Boy: Are you still travelling to the seaside by coach? I could meet you when you arrive. || Girl: I planned to, but all the seats have already been booked. I considered going by car, but your parents need it for work. [1]I am going by train instead[/1]. || Boy: Good idea. What time will you get there? || Girl: Just before eleven, if everything is on time. I have already checked the timetable online, so it should be easy. I will message you when I leave, so you do not have to wait outside.
items.question: How will the speaker get to the seaside?
items.explanation: The speaker will take the train.

items.context: You hear two students talking about supplies for art class.
items.audio_script: Girl: Did you buy some paint for the art lesson? I saw one in the shop window yesterday and thought it looked nice. || Boy: No. I looked at some paint, and I nearly chose some coloured paper, but the teacher has paint and paper but my old glue has dried up. [1]I bought a bottle of glue in the end[/1]. || Girl: Good choice. It sounds more useful, and I think it will be appreciated. || Boy: I hope so. I kept the receipt, just in case it needs changing after we give it.
items.question: What did the student buy?
